“Oooh, this looks similar to Hazbin Hotel.” “Oooh, there’s a demon voiced by Keith David.” 

I understand the comparison; after all, there is an adult animated show featuring a hotel inhabited by demons. However, it’s important to recognise that the similarities end there.

**Hazbin Hotel** tells the story of the Princess of Hell, who is trying to rehabilitate sinners to save them from the horrors of the annual extermination sent by Heaven. Unfortunately, the sinners she is attempting to save have no interest in becoming good and prefer to remain as they are, even at the expense of their souls.

In contrast, **Haunted Hotel** follows a single mother who inherits a hotel from her deceased brother. She is simply trying to run a business while supporting her children and dealing with the supernatural events that occur, all while striving to make ends meet.

It’s possible that this is just part of the rivalry between two competing streaming services, and Netflix may be attempting to capitalise on the popularity of Hazbin Hotel. However, if we focus too much on the similarities, we might miss the enjoyment that’s right in front of us. Therefore, while we await the next season of Hazbin Hotel, let’s make sure to enjoy Haunted Hotel when it premieres.
